rules.description: String interpolation in SQL query. User input directly concatenated into SQL statement.
rules.fix: Use parameterized queries: cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id=%s', (user_id,))

rules.description: String concatenation in cursor.execute(). User input appended to SQL query.
rules.fix: Pass parameters as second argument to execute(): cursor.execute(query, params)

rules.description: Potential string formatting in SQL query construction.
subprocess with shell=True allows command injection via shell metacharacters.
rules.fix: Use subprocess.run(['cmd', arg]) without shell=True, and validate input with allowlist.

rules.description: os.system() or os.popen() with user input allows arbitrary command execution.
rules.fix: Replace with subprocess.run() and never pass user input directly.

rules.description: Jinja2 | safe filter disables auto-escaping. User input rendered as raw HTML.
rules.fix: Remove | safe filter. Use escape() or let Jinja2 auto-escape by default.

rules.description: render_template_string() with user input can lead to SSTI or XSS if not escaped.
rules.fix: Use render_template() with separate template files. Escape user input with markupsafe.

rules.description: User input marked as safe without sanitization.
rules.fix: Escape all user input before rendering. Only mark as safe after sanitization.

rules.description: File opened with user-supplied path. Allows reading arbitrary files (../../etc/passwd).
rules.fix: Use os.path.basename() to strip path components. Restrict to whitelist of allowed files.

rules.description: os.path.join with user input. Attacker can use absolute paths to escape the directory.
rules.fix: Validate the result is still within the intended directory using os.path.realpath().

rules.description: eval() or exec() with user input allows arbitrary Python code execution on the server.
rules.fix: Never use eval()/exec() with user input. Use safe parsers like ast.literal_eval() for data.

rules.description: Unpickling user-supplied data allows remote code execution.
rules.fix: Never unpickle data from untrusted sources. Use JSON or a safe serialization format.

rules.description: Hardcoded credentials or secrets in source code. Exposed if code is leaked.
rules.fix: Use environment variables: os.environ.get('SECRET_KEY'). Never commit secrets to version control.

rules.description: Debug mode enabled. Exposes stack traces, environment variables, and interactive debugger.
rules.fix: Set debug=False in production. Use environment variable: app.run(debug=os.environ.get('DEBUG','0')=='1')
